items.title: Beatbot AquaSense X Review: A Pool Robot That Cleans Itself
items.summary: The AquaSense X brings self-cleaning technology to pool robots for the first time, but is it worth nearly twice the price of Beatbot’s flagship cleaner?

items.title: Skylight’s Touchscreen Calendar Got my Whole Family on the Same Page
items.summary: The Skylight has become the informational and organizational hub of my household. My touchscreen-native kids have also gained more agency over our family activities.

items.title: Samsung Micro RGB R95H Review (2026): Not the Brightest
items.summary: There’s a new fleet of TVs using new mini and micro RBG display tech, and Samsung’s R95H model isn’t as impressive as it should be.

items.title: This slushie machine was a lifesaver during NYC’s heat wave
items.summary: Last weekend’s brutal NYC heat wave had me craving a frozen drink almost every afternoon. Normally, that would mean sweating through a walk to 7-Eleven for a slurpee. This time, though, I stayed home and put the new Ninja Slushi Twist to the test. Ninja’s latest slushie machine builds on the popularity of the original […]

items.title: Smart glasses without a camera? Even Realities bets productivity beats recording everyone
items.summary: The glasses are targeted at people who might be constantly in meetings, giving presentations, and traveling to countries where different languages are spoken.

items.title: OpenAI bets on families as ChatGPT goes deeper into households
items.summary: ChatGPT is hiring a dedicated product manager to build experiences for families, caregivers, and older adults, according to a job posting.

items.title: China's second typhoon in a week makes landfall
items.summary: Nearly two million people are evacuated from eastern Zhejiang province, with the city of Wenzhou close to the path of the storm.

items.title: Iran supreme leader calls for revenge for father's killing
items.summary: Mojtaba Khamenei says vengeance is "inevitable" for the killing, which happened on the first day of the US-Israeli war on Iran.

items.title: Spain battles to contain one of its deadliest wildfires as at least 12 killed
items.summary: At least four Britons are believed to be among the victims of the blaze, which left another 23 people missing.

items.summary: Federal Reserve Chairman Kevin Warsh on Thursday released names of the experts who will comprise five task forces to examine the institution's operations.

items.title: Prediction markets spark insider trading concerns. Here's how Goldman and other companies are responding
items.summary: CNBC reached out to 50 companies about what are their trading policies for employees on prediction markets. A handful had an answer.

items.title: Stocks Sink in Broad AI Rout Sparked by China's DeepSeek
items.summary: U.S. stocks were mostly lower, with the Nasdaq leading declines as makers of AI infrastructure suffered steep falls, many in the double digits. Nvidia was down 16%.

items.summary: Gold settled 1.4% lower, down two of the past three sessions, and silver fell 2.5%, down three of the past four sessions.

items.summary: A cryptocurrency regulation bill is facing a make-or-break moment as senators seek to resolve remaining policy disputes in the next four-week stretch, which experts warn is likely the last window to pass the legislation before the midterm elections. Bipartisan negotiations over the Clarity Act, which aims to provide a regulatory framework for the digital assets…

items.summary: President Trump’s trip to Turkey this week for the NATO alliance’s summit made big headlines over his comments about the state of U.S.-Iranian negotiations and security risks posed by the Iran war. items.title: Second pregnancy changes the brain in surprising new ways
items.summary: Researchers found that every pregnancy rewires the brain in its own way, with a second pregnancy bringing a different pattern of changes than the first. The discoveries could lead to better ways to recognize and treat maternal mental health challenges, including peripartum depression.

items.title: Physicists finally build a quantum material predicted more than a decade ago
items.summary: Researchers have achieved a major milestone by creating a long-sought two-dimensional quantum material and confirming its unusual conducting edge states. The ability to control these states through strain could make the material a promising platform for future room-temperature quantum electronics.

items.title: Experimental drug reverses severe fatty liver disease by repairing the gut
items.summary: An experimental drug called DT-109 reversed severe fatty liver disease in animal studies by repairing the gut and preventing harmful toxins from damaging the liver. The discovery could open the door to a new class of treatments for MASH and potentially other diseases tied to gut health.

items.title: Pluto has landslides
items.summary: New Horizons data reveal Pluto’s first six confirmed landslides along steep crater rims.

items.title: Here’s what happens when you put politicians in charge of science
items.summary: Proposed federal rules would rely on political appointees to decide how a lot of U.S. science gets done. History shows the consequences of such actions.

items.title: Lost in Curiosity reveals the messy reality of doing science
items.summary: In her new book, science journalist Roberta Kwok takes readers behind the scenes to understand how researchers get nature to give up its secrets.
